The author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck. They need to determine the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards dealt. This can be calculated using combinatorial functions. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from the deck is $ \binom{52}{5} $. The number of ways to choose 5 cards with no hearts is $ \binom{36}{5} $, since there are 36 non-heart cards in the deck. The probability of getting no hearts in the 5 cards is $ \frac{\binom{36}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}} $. Therefore, the probability of getting at least one heart is $ 1 - \frac{\binom{36}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}} $. This expression is the closed form of the probability.

*** 

The author has two strategies to consider. Strategy A has an expected value of $ 0.4 \times 1000 - 0.6 \times 500 = 400 - 300 = 100 $. Strategy B has an expected value of $ 0.6 \times 800 - 0.4 \times 200 = 480 - 80 = 400 $. The author should choose Strategy B, as it has a higher expected value. The phrase "bag of tricks" is a palindrome that fits the requirement.
